```{=html}
<aside class="side-panel identities-sidebar">
<details open>
<summary>Closed-form identities</summary>
<dl>
  <dt>Shared kernel</dt>
  <dd><code>I<sub>T</sub> = ∫₀<sup>T</sup> S<sub>t</sub> dt</code>.
  Every stochastic book is linear or affine in it; Dufresne (2001)
  gives the first two moments in closed form.</dd>

  <dt>Fee ↔ back-to-back variance</dt>
  <dd><code>Var[Π<sub>b2b</sub>] / Var[R<sub>fee</sub>] = (P / f)²</code>
  — shared kernel, opposite signs, identical shape up to scale.</dd>

  <dt>Break-even quote</dt>
  <dd><code>Q* = (1 + f) · P · S<sub>0</sub> · (e<sup>μT</sup> − 1) / (μT)</code>.
  Equalises <code>E[·]</code> of fee and back-to-back, not their
  distributions.</dd>

  <dt>Partial scaling</dt>
  <dd>SD and tail quantiles collapse as <code>(1 − α)</code>; at
  <code>α = 1</code> the book is deterministic.</dd>

  <dt>Syndicated scaling</dt>
  <dd><code>Var[Π<sub>ret</sub>] = (1 − α)²(1 − β)² · Var[Π<sub>b2b</sub>]</code>.
  At the fair premium (<code>θ = 0</code>), cession preserves mean.</dd>

  <dt>Switching</dt>
  <dd>Flip at <code>τ = inf{t : S<sub>t</sub> ≥ h · S<sub>0</sub>}</code>.
  No closed-form density; truncates rather than rescales the loss tail.</dd>

  <dt>Merton compensation</dt>
  <dd>Drift absorbs <code>−λ<sub>J</sub> · κ</code> with
  <code>κ = e<sup>μ<sub>J</sub> + σ<sub>J</sub>²/2</sup> − 1</code>,
  so <code>E[S<sub>t</sub>] = S<sub>0</sub> · e<sup>μt</sup></code>.
  Means survive jumps; variances do not.</dd>
</dl>
</details>
</aside>
```
